channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Tandem bulk oxygen diffusion and surface reactions in reducible metal oxides control redox cycle dynamics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71787-0

The interplay between bulk oxygen diffusion and surface reactions in reducible metal oxides is key in heterogeneous catalysts, yet direct measurements of their coupling through transient kinetics and in situ spectroscopies have been lacking. Here, the authors uncover complex H₂-driven dynamics in ceria–zirconia using transient mass spectrometry along with in-situ Raman, near-ambient pressure X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, machine learning potential-based molecular simulations, and multiscale kinetic modeling.
item.dc:title: Tandem bulk oxygen diffusion and surface reactions in reducible metal oxides control redox cycle dynamics
item.dc:creator: Quentin Kim
George Yan
Alfred Worrad
Sanjana Srinivas
Sinmyung Yoon
Sagar Sourav
J. Anibal Boscoboinik
Weiqing Zheng
Dionisios G. Vlachos

item.title: Author Correction: Operando monitoring of the functional role of tetrahedral cobalt centers for the oxygen evolution reaction
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71974-z

Author Correction: Operando monitoring of the functional role of tetrahedral cobalt centers for the oxygen evolution reaction
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Operando monitoring of the functional role of tetrahedral cobalt centers for the oxygen evolution reaction

Nanchen Dongfang

Jingguo Li

item.title: Author Correction: Advances, challenges and prospects of origami and kirigami optoelectronics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71982-z

Author Correction: Advances, challenges and prospects of origami and kirigami optoelectronics
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Advances, challenges and prospects of origami and kirigami optoelectronics
item.dc:creator: Donggyun Lee
Su-Bon Kim
Junho Kim
Seunghyup Yoo

item.title: Unconventional magnon-mediated spin torque enabled by ferroelectric domain engineering in multiferroic BiFeO<sub>3</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71437-5

Conventional spin-orbit torques are limited by symmetry. Here, the ferroelectric domain structure of multiferroic bismuth ferrite is engineered to manipulate the orientation of magnon spin current, enabling field-free type-x magnetic switching.
item.dc:title: Unconventional magnon-mediated spin torque enabled by ferroelectric domain engineering in multiferroic BiFeO<sub>3</sub>
item.dc:creator: Yuhan Liang
Xingyu Yan
Xiaoyu Jiang
Dingsong Jiang
Hao Bai
Zi-An Wang
Hetian Chen
Xiaofu Qiu
Daniel Pharis
Xiaoxi Huang
Rakshit Jain
Dingfu Shao
Wanjun Jiang
Di Yi
Daniel C. Ralph
Tianxiang Nan

item.title: Structural motifs of gold cluster anions with 17 to 69 atoms
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71649-9

A combination of DFT calculations, comprehensive structural searches and high-resolution photoelectron spectra unveils a broad variety of often highly symmetric structural motifs of gold cluster anions with a few tens of atoms.
item.dc:title: Structural motifs of gold cluster anions with 17 to 69 atoms
item.dc:creator: Andrés Aguado
Pablo Álvarez-Zapatero
Oleg Kostko
Bernd von Issendorff
